conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 0 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resource. Goal: Maximize profit (recover cost + gain surplus). </my goals>
<reason>
- Current cost to produce X: 40 ZUP.
- Only have 1 unit of X for sale.
- Need to cover cost (40 ZUP) and aim for surplus.
- Player BLUE has ZUP but no visible resources, so must rely on their willingness to pay.
- Since I can only propose 3 times, I need to start with a reasonable but strategic offer to gauge their response. Starting mid-range to allow room for negotiation and profit.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I am offering 1 unit of X for 60 ZUP. This allows me to recover the cost (40 ZUP) while securing a surplus of 20 ZUP.
What is your counteroffer?
</message>

<other player message> I am willing to trade 20 units of X for 40 ZUP. This is a fair trade considering my goal is to acquire the X at the lowest possible cost. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 20 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resource. Goal: Maximize profit (recover cost + gain surplus). </my goals>
<reason>
- Player BLUE is proposing a trade for **20 units of X for 40 ZUP**, but I only have **1 unit of X available**.
- Their goal is to acquire X at the lowest possible cost, but I cannot fulfill their request for 20 units.
- This trade is unfeasible for me because I only have **1 unit**, and even if I accept, it would not benefit me strategically (I cannot produce more X).
- However, I can still negotiate a fair price for the **single unit** I have.
- If I accept their proposal as-is, I would lose the opportunity to sell my unit at a better price.
- If I reject, I forfeit the chance to trade entirely.
- Their proposal implies they expect a discount for bulk, but since I cannot fulfill bulk, I need to adjust expectations.
- A reasonable counteroffer would be to ask for a higher price per unit (e.g., 60 ZUP for 1 X), as they are trying to acquire X cheaply.
- Since this is my second interaction with their proposal, I should firm up my position: I will not sell more than 1 X.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 70 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I only have **1 unit of X** for sale, not 20.
I am adjusting my offer to **70 ZUP for 1 X**, as I cannot fulfill bulk orders.
This allows me to recover my production cost (40 ZUP) and still secure a surplus (30 ZUP).
Please reconsider this offer.
</message>
